title: James Mirrlees auction markets in everything - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: Tyler Cowen flags a Nuffield College charity auction of James Mirrlees' personal book collection using a simplified version of Paul Klemperer's Product-Mix Auction — the same mechanism the Bank of England has used since the 2007-08 financial crisis to sell nearly £400 billion of repos — with proceeds funding scholarships in Mirrlees' memory.

insights.claim: The Bank of England has used Paul Klemperer's Product-Mix Auction, designed in 2007-08 at the onset of the financial crisis, to sell almost £400 billion of repos, and continues to run it regularly.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Quantifies the real-world scale at which this auction-design mechanism has operated inside a G7 central bank's crisis-era liquidity operations, a concrete market-design case study.
insights.quote: The Bank still runs its version regularly, and has now sold almost £400 billion of repos using it.

insights.claim: Nuffield College is auctioning signed books and items from James Mirrlees' personal collection using a simplified version of the same Product-Mix Auction mechanism originally designed for Bank of England monetary operations, showing the format's flexibility beyond its original use case.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: An example of a monetary-policy market-design tool being ported to an unrelated small-scale charity context, illustrating the general portability of well-designed auction mechanisms.
insights.quote: Our auction is a simplification of the Product-Mix Auction that Paul Klemperer... designed for the Bank of England in 2007-8

insights.claim: The Nuffield College book auction is deliberately designed to maximize 'social welfare' rather than expected revenue, a design choice enabled by the flexibility of the Product-Mix Auction format compared to simple revenue-maximizing auction formats.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Highlights that auction mechanism choice can explicitly target objectives other than revenue maximization, relevant background for anyone evaluating or designing allocation mechanisms.
insights.quote: The auction is focused on “social welfare”, not expected revenue…
